rulings.Case Title: VASILIKI SERVETA VS. KEVIN J. HESLIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 5, 2022 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Alter ego allegations are sufficient providing the basis for sufficiency of allegations for all causes of action as to the moving defendants. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JEREMY ZHI EN CHEW VS. MARCO MEJIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Claimants Keny Lopez And Reina Ramirez'S Motion Under Ccp Sec 473 For Relief From Expiration Of Time To File Prejudgment Claim And To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Recall Writ Of Possession; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 5, 2022 line 5. NOTICE OF CLAIMANTS KENY LOPEZ AND REINA RAMIREZ'S MOTION UNDER CCP SECTION 473 FOR RELIEF FROM EXPIRATION OF TIME TO FILE PREJUDGMENT CLAIM AND TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND RECALL WRIT OF POSSESSION is GRANTED in part and MOOT in part. Default judgment is stricken by the Court. It was entered due to a clerical error as UD-120 filed on April 22, 2022 is incomplete. Moving claimants are granted leave to file pre-judgment claims no later than July 5, 2022 and responses in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendant'S Motion To Vacate Judgment And Recall And Quash The Writ Of Execution
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 5, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT AND RECALL AND QUASH THE WRIT OF EXECUTION is transferred to department 220 to be heard by the honorable Kathleen Kelly on July 6, 2022 at 9:30 a.m. Mr. Singer and Ms. Rochette to be present in-person at July 6, 2022 hearing. The Court will grant a one week stay of execution on July 5, 2022. Defendant shall appear for hearing on July 5, 2022 at 9:30 a.m. with two copies of proposed order for stay. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
